(**CBD**) is the [commercial](/wiki/Commerce "Commerce") and [business](/wiki/Business "Business") center of a city. It contains commercial space and offices, and in larger cities will often be described as a [financial district](/wiki/Financial_district "Financial district"). Geographically, it often coincides with the "[city centre](/wiki/City_centre "City centre")" or "[downtown](/wiki/Downtown "Downtown")". However, these concepts are not necessarily synonymous: many cities have a **central *business* district** located away from its traditional city center, and there may be multiple CBDs within a single urban area. The CBD will often be highly accessible and have a large variety and concentration of specialised goods and services compared to other parts of the city.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

A **central business district** (**CBD**) is the [commercial](/wiki/Commerce "Commerce") and [business](/wiki/Business "Business") center of a city. It contains commercial space and offices, and in larger cities will often be described as a [financial district](/wiki/Financial_district "Financial district"). Geographically, it often coincides with the "[city centre](/wiki/City_centre "City centre")" or "[downtown](/wiki/Downtown "Downtown")". However, these concepts are not necessarily synonymous: many cities have a **central *business* district** located away from its traditional city center, and there may be multiple CBDs within a single urban area. The CBD will often be highly accessible and have a large variety and concentration of specialised goods and services compared to other parts of the city.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

In [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), the [Chicago Loop](/wiki/Chicago_Loop "Chicago Loop") is the second-largest central business district in the United States. It is also referred to as the core of the city's downtown.

[Mexico City](/wiki/Mexico_City "Mexico City") also has its own [historic city center](/wiki/Historic_center_of_Mexico_City "Historic center of Mexico City"), the colonial era "Centro Histórico", along with two CBDs: the mid-late 20th century [Paseo de la Reforma](/wiki/Paseo_de_la_Reforma "Paseo de la Reforma") in [Polanco](/wiki/Polanco "Polanco"), and the new [Santa Fe](/wiki/Santa_Fe_(Mexico_City) "Santa Fe (Mexico City)"), respectively. [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia")'s largest central business district is the [Moscow International Business Center](/wiki/Moscow_International_Business_Center "Moscow International Business Center") in [Moscow](/wiki/Moscow "Moscow").

The shape and type of a central business district almost always closely reflect the city's history. Cities with strong [preservation](/wiki/Historic_preservation "Historic preservation") laws and maximum building height restrictions to retain the character of the historic and cultural core may have a CBD quite a distance from the city centre (and in some cases, outside the city limits itself). This distinction is quite common in European cities such as: [London](/wiki/London "London"), [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), [Moscow](/wiki/Moscow "Moscow"), [Vienna](/wiki/Vienna "Vienna"), [Prague](/wiki/Prague "Prague") and [Budapest](/wiki/Budapest "Budapest"). The [New World](/wiki/New_World "New World") grew quickly after the emergence of modern transport, therefore a single centre often included many of the region's tallest buildings and served as both a commercial and cultural city centre.

In the 21st century, increasing [urbanisation](/wiki/Urbanisation "Urbanisation") has led to the development of [megacities](/wiki/Megacity "Megacity") that often have multiple CBDs scattered across the urban area. Downtown sections of cities, especially in [North America](/wiki/North_America "North America"), often are distinct from CBDs and city centres.[[2]](#cite_note-2) No two CBDs have the same spatial shape, but there are certain common geometric patterns, which are largely a result of centralised commercial and industrial activities.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Australia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Australia")]

In [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"), the term CBD is widely used officially and colloquially, in the sense of city centre. The three biggest cities, [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney"), [Melbourne](/wiki/Melbourne "Melbourne") and [Brisbane](/wiki/Brisbane "Brisbane")[[4]](#cite_note-4) have large CBDs. Sydney features growing micro central business districts, which serve as the hub for their respective areas outside the CBD. An example is [Parramatta](/wiki/Parramatta "Parramatta") (which is considered the financial hub of [Western Sydney](/wiki/Western_Sydney "Western Sydney")), [Chatswood](/wiki/Chatswood,_New_South_Wales "Chatswood, New South Wales") and [North Sydney](/wiki/North_Sydney "North Sydney"). Also on the outskirts of Brisbane, within *Brisbane-Gold Coast urban area* with a population of three million,[[5]](#cite_note-demographia-5) a new micro [central business district](/wiki/Gold_Coast_central_business_district "Gold Coast central business district") being created in [Southport](/wiki/Southport,_Queensland "Southport, Queensland").

## Brazil

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Brazil")]

[São Paulo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo "São Paulo") is a multipolar city, with several business districts. [Avenida Faria Lima](/wiki/Avenida_Faria_Lima "Avenida Faria Lima") and the neighboring districts, [Itaim Bibi](/wiki/Itaim_Bibi "Itaim Bibi") and [Vila Olímpia](/wiki/Vila_Ol%C3%ADmpia "Vila Olímpia"), now concentrate a large part of the financial activities in São Paulo and [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"). [Avenida Paulista](/wiki/Avenida_Paulista "Avenida Paulista") and nearby streets is a well-established financial center that has several banking offices and corporate headquarters, as well as hospitals. Its [Historic Center](/wiki/Historic_Center_of_S%C3%A3o_Paulo "Historic Center of São Paulo") was the city's first business center; There, the [São Paulo Stock Exchange](/wiki/B3_(stock_exchange) "B3 (stock exchange)") is located. The region comprising Avenidas Berrini, Doutor Chucri Zaidan, and Nações Unidas, the city's newest commercial district, has offices of multinational companies and also commercial services.

In [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), Brazil's second-largest city, the main business district still is in downtown, where [Petrobrás](/wiki/Petrobr%C3%A1s "Petrobrás") and Vale headquarters are located, but there is a concentration of business in [Botafogo](/wiki/Botafogo "Botafogo") harbor and in [Barra da Tijuca](/wiki/Barra_da_Tijuca "Barra da Tijuca"), a newer Rio de Janeiro suburb located in city's west region.

## Colombia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Colombia")]

The largest CBD in [Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia") is [Bogotá](/wiki/Bogot%C3%A1 "Bogotá")'s *Centro Internacional*, where some of the tallest buildings in [South America](/wiki/South_America "South America") have been repurposed as national headquarters for [Scotiabank Colpatria](/wiki/Scotiabank "Scotiabank"), [Davivienda](/wiki/Davivienda "Davivienda"), and [Bancolombia](/wiki/Bancolombia "Bancolombia"), among others.

Over the years, Bogotá has developed minor business districts, which include *Avenida Chile*, *Ciudad Salitre*, with companies like [Avianca](/wiki/Avianca "Avianca"), [Rappi](/wiki/Rappi "Rappi"), [Johnson & Johnson](/wiki/Johnson_%26_Johnson "Johnson & Johnson"), [Terpel](/wiki/Terpel "Terpel"), [Hilton](/wiki/Hilton_Hotels_%26_Resorts "Hilton Hotels & Resorts"), [Hyatt](/wiki/Hyatt "Hyatt"), [Marriott](/wiki/Marriott_International "Marriott International"), [Sheraton](/wiki/Sheraton_Hotels_and_Resorts "Sheraton Hotels and Resorts"), [Ramada](/wiki/Ramada "Ramada"), [City Express](/wiki/City_Express "City Express"), [Aloft](/wiki/Aloft "Aloft") and [Novotel](/wiki/Novotel "Novotel"); or *Complejo Santa Bárbara*, the last of which features the only [W Hotel](/wiki/W_Hotel "W Hotel") in the city.

Colombia's other notable CBDs include *Parque Berrío* in [Medellín](/wiki/Medell%C3%ADn "Medellín"), *[Bocagrande](/wiki/Bocagrande "Bocagrande")* in [Cartagena](/wiki/Cartagena_de_Indias "Cartagena de Indias") (the largest touristic CBD in the country), and *Paseo de Bolívar* in [Barranquilla](/wiki/Barranquilla "Barranquilla").

## France

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: France")]

[La Défense](/wiki/La_D%C3%A9fense "La Défense"), west of [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), is [France](/wiki/France "France")'s largest central business district. As of 2025, La Défense was Europe's largest purpose built business district.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

La Défense hosts 19 of the [world's biggest 500 companies](/wiki/Fortune_Global_500 "Fortune Global 500"), whilst the [Paris region](/wiki/Ile-de-France "Ile-de-France") as a whole hosts 29 of the world's 500 largest companies, establishing itself as the first city in Europe (and the third worldwide) for the number of companies classified in *[Fortune](/wiki/Fortune_(magazine) "Fortune (magazine)")* [Fortune Global 500](/wiki/Fortune_Global_500 "Fortune Global 500").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

  [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris")
* [![Lyon](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/97/Lyon-part-dieu-2023.jpg/330px-Lyon-part-dieu-2023.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lyon-part-dieu-2023.jpg "Lyon")

  [Lyon](/wiki/Lyon "Lyon")

[La Part-Dieu](/wiki/La_Part-Dieu "La Part-Dieu") (French: [la paʁdjø]) is a central business district in the 3rd arrondissement of [Lyon](/wiki/Lyon "Lyon"), France. It is the second-largest tertiary district in France, after La Défense in Greater Paris. The area also contains Lyon's principal railway station, [Gare de La Part-Dieu](/wiki/Lyon-Part-Dieu_station "Lyon-Part-Dieu station").

## Germany

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Germany")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9d/Frankfurt_Am_Main-Stadtansicht_von_der_Deutschherrnbruecke_zu_Beginn_der_Abenddaemmerung.jpg/250px-Frankfurt_Am_Main-Stadtansicht_von_der_Deutschherrnbruecke_zu_Beginn_der_Abenddaemmerung.jpg)](/wiki/File:Frankfurt_Am_Main-Stadtansicht_von_der_Deutschherrnbruecke_zu_Beginn_der_Abenddaemmerung.jpg)

Central business district of [Frankfurt](/wiki/Frankfurt "Frankfurt"), Germany

In [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"), the terms *Innenstadt* and *Stadtzentrum* may be used to describe the central business district. These terms can be literally translated as "[inner city](/wiki/Inner_city "Inner city")" and "city center". Some of the [larger cities](/wiki/Metropolitan_regions_in_Germany "Metropolitan regions in Germany") have more than one central business district. For example, [Berlin](/wiki/Berlin "Berlin") alone has three.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Due to Berlin's history of division during the [Cold War](/wiki/Cold_War "Cold War"), the city contains central business districts both in West ([Kurfürstendamm](/wiki/Kurf%C3%BCrstendamm "Kurfürstendamm")) and in East Berlin ([Alexanderplatz](/wiki/Alexanderplatz "Alexanderplatz")), as well as a newly built business district near the [Potsdamer Platz](/wiki/Potsdamer_Platz "Potsdamer Platz"). The city's [historic center](/wiki/Mitte_(locality) "Mitte (locality)") (home to the [Reichstag building](/wiki/Reichstag_building "Reichstag building"), as well as the [Brandenburg Gate](/wiki/Brandenburg_Gate "Brandenburg Gate") and most federal ministries) was largely abandoned when the [Berlin Wall](/wiki/Berlin_Wall "Berlin Wall") cut through the area. Only after the reunification with the redevelopment of [Potsdamer Platz](/wiki/Potsdamer_Platz "Potsdamer Platz"), and the construction of numerous shopping centers, government ministries, embassies, office buildings and entertainment venues, was the area revived.

In [Frankfurt am Main](/wiki/Frankfurt "Frankfurt")'s city center, there is a business district called the "[Bankenviertel](/wiki/Bankenviertel "Bankenviertel")".

In [Düsseldorf](/wiki/D%C3%BCsseldorf "Düsseldorf"), there is a business district which is located around the famous high street [Königsallee](/wiki/K%C3%B6nigsallee "Königsallee") with banks, shops and offices.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Hong Kong

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Hong Kong")]

Traditionally, the Central Business District "CBD" of [Hong Kong](/wiki/Hong_Kong "Hong Kong") is [Central](/wiki/Central,_Hong_Kong "Central, Hong Kong"), where many multinational [financial services corporations](/wiki/Financial_institution "Financial institution") have their headquarters.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Consulates general and consulates of many countries are also located in this area, as is [Government Hill](/wiki/Government_Hill "Government Hill"), the site of the old government headquarters.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] As the Hong Kong SAR Government Headquarters moved to Tamar, Admiralty, the nearby area is also considered part of the CBD.

In [Quarry Bay](/wiki/Quarry_Bay "Quarry Bay"), [Taikoo Place](/wiki/Taikoo_Place "Taikoo Place") and areas nearby, including [Admiralty](/wiki/Admiralty,_Hong_Kong "Admiralty, Hong Kong"), [Wan Chai](/wiki/Wan_Chai "Wan Chai") and [Causeway Bay](/wiki/Causeway_Bay "Causeway Bay"), and [Tsim Sha Tsui](/wiki/Tsim_Sha_Tsui "Tsim Sha Tsui"), [Kwun Tong](/wiki/Kwun_Tong "Kwun Tong"), and [Kowloon Bay](/wiki/Kowloon_Bay "Kowloon Bay") are regarded as major business districts of Hong Kong.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## India

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: India")]

[India](/wiki/India "India")'s capital [New Delhi](/wiki/New_Delhi "New Delhi") has been known for its CBD at [Connaught Place](/wiki/Connaught_Place,_New_Delhi "Connaught Place, New Delhi"). For the financial capital of the country, the famous CBD is [The Bandra Kurla Complex](/wiki/Bandra_Kurla_Complex "Bandra Kurla Complex") in [Mumbai](/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai"). In [Mumbai](/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai") itself there is [Nariman Point](/wiki/Nariman_Point "Nariman Point"), which is another CBD including [Andheri](/wiki/Andheri "Andheri") and [Parel](/wiki/Parel "Parel"). [Parry's Corner](/wiki/Parry%27s_Corner "Parry's Corner") and [Nungambakkam](/wiki/Nungambakkam "Nungambakkam") are among the main CBDs of [Chennai](/wiki/Chennai "Chennai").

[Bangalore](/wiki/Bangalore "Bangalore") has five notable business districts: [UB City](/wiki/UB_City "UB City"), Brigade Gateway, [Koramangala](/wiki/Koramangala "Koramangala"), [Indiranagar](/wiki/Indiranagar,_Bangalore "Indiranagar, Bangalore"), and [Electronic City](/wiki/Electronic_City "Electronic City"). [Lucknow](/wiki/Lucknow "Lucknow") has three business districts, including [Hazratganj Market](/wiki/Hazratganj "Hazratganj"), Gomti Nagar Extension at the extended area of [Gomti Nagar](/wiki/Gomti_Nagar "Gomti Nagar"), and [Aminabad](/wiki/Aminabad,_Lucknow "Aminabad, Lucknow") in old Lucknow. [Kolkata](/wiki/Kolkata "Kolkata") also has central business districts, including [B.B.D. Bagh](/wiki/B.B.D._Bagh "B.B.D. Bagh"), [Park Circus](/wiki/Park_Circus "Park Circus"), [Esplanade](/wiki/Esplanade,_Kolkata "Esplanade, Kolkata"), [Salt Lake Sector V](/wiki/Salt_Lake_Sector_V "Salt Lake Sector V"), and [New Town](/wiki/New_Town,_Kolkata "New Town, Kolkata").

## Indonesia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Indonesia")]

The largest CBD in [Indonesia](/wiki/Indonesia "Indonesia") is the [Golden Triangle](/wiki/Golden_Triangle_of_Jakarta "Golden Triangle of Jakarta") (*Segitiga Emas* in [Indonesian](/wiki/Indonesian_language "Indonesian language")) in [Jakarta](/wiki/Jakarta "Jakarta"). [Sudirman Central Business District](/wiki/SCBD "SCBD"), a super block that is located within the Golden Triangle, is the first of its kind in Indonesia, and one of the largest commercial center developments in the city. Jakarta started developing its business district in the early 1960s before hosting the [Asian Games in 1962](/wiki/1962_Asian_Games "1962 Asian Games").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

[Surabaya](/wiki/Surabaya "Surabaya") in [East Java](/wiki/East_Java "East Java") built its first central business district in the Darmo region. The construction was expected to be completed by 2018 with 150 SOHO units and 500 residences.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Italy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Italy")]

In [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"), business districts do not coincide with the geographical centers of the city because the city center is the historical center of the city, and usually not very suitable to function as a modern business district.[*[original research?](/wiki/Wikipedia:No_original_research "Wikipedia:No original research")*]

A precursor to modern business districts is [EUR](/wiki/EUR,_Rome "EUR, Rome") in [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"), which is now home to several national and international companies and public bodies.

The EUR, the [Napoli business district](/wiki/Centro_direzionale_di_Napoli "Centro direzionale di Napoli"), the [Milan business district](/wiki/Centro_Direzionale_di_Milano "Centro Direzionale di Milano"), and [City Life](/wiki/CityLife_(Milan) "CityLife (Milan)"), are among the most important commercial areas in Italy. Other important business and financial centers are present in [Brescia](/wiki/Brescia_Due "Brescia Due"), [Genoa](/wiki/Corte_Lambruschini "Corte Lambruschini") and [Reggio Calabria](/w/index.php?title=Centro_Direzionale_di_Reggio_Calabria&action=edit&redlink=1 "Centro Direzionale di Reggio Calabria (page does not exist)").

## Kazakhstan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Kazakhstan")]

In [Kazakhstan](/wiki/Kazakhstan "Kazakhstan"), central business districts are concentrated primarily in [Almaty](/wiki/Almaty "Almaty") and [Astana](/wiki/Astana "Astana"), the country's largest cities. Almaty, as the former capital and financial hub, hosts the majority of national and international corporate offices, while Astana, the political center, has been developing modern commercial areas alongside government buildings.

In [Almaty](/wiki/Almaty "Almaty"), the [Nurly Tau Business Center](/wiki/Nurly_Tau_Business_Center "Nurly Tau Business Center") serves as a multifunctional hub, combining office space, residential units, and leisure facilities. Covering approximately 300,000 m², it is organized in a "city within a city" layout with class A offices, entertainment spaces, and underground parking.[[11]](#cite_note-:1-11) Other business districts in Almaty include the [Esentai Tower](/wiki/Esentai_Tower "Esentai Tower") and areas along Kabanbai Batyr Avenue, which host financial institutions and corporate headquarters.

Astana’s main commercial district is the Central Business District, featuring government buildings, banks, and modern office towers, designed to support the city’s rapid urban expansion and status as the national capital.

## Pakistan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Pakistan")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/74/Views_of_Karachi.jpg/250px-Views_of_Karachi.jpg)](/wiki/File:Views_of_Karachi.jpg)

[Clifton, Karachi](/wiki/Clifton,_Karachi "Clifton, Karachi") in Pakistan

[![3D render of Pakistan's first Central Business District currently under construction.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3b/DAP_Walton_BEV01-3.jpg/250px-DAP_Walton_BEV01-3.jpg)](/wiki/File:DAP_Walton_BEV01-3.jpg)

[Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan")'s first central business district under construction

In Pakistan, a central business district or a large, concentrated urban setting within a settlement is called a [shehar](/w/index.php?title=Shehar&action=edit&redlink=1 "Shehar (page does not exist)"). [Karachi](/wiki/Karachi "Karachi") is Pakistan's largest city and the country's economic hub; the [I. I. Chundrigar Road](/wiki/I._I._Chundrigar_Road "I. I. Chundrigar Road") acts as Karachi's main financial district. [Shahra-e-Faisal](/wiki/Shahrah-e-Faisal "Shahrah-e-Faisal") in Karachi is also one of the most important business districts of Pakistan.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

[Gulberg, Lahore](/wiki/Gulberg,_Lahore "Gulberg, Lahore") has a large number of important office buildings as well as many high-rises and shopping malls. [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") established its first central business district, Lahore Central Business District, also known as Central Business District Punjab (CBD Punjab) by an ACT of Parliament (LCBDDA Act, 2021) in February 2021.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Jinnah Avenue in [Islamabad](/wiki/Islamabad "Islamabad") is the main business district of the city. [Blue Area](/wiki/Blue_Area "Blue Area") is the central business district of [Islamabad](/wiki/Islamabad "Islamabad").

[D Ground](/wiki/D_Ground "D Ground") is the central business district of [Faisalabad](/wiki/Faisalabad "Faisalabad") and [Saddar](/wiki/Saddar,_Rawalpindi "Saddar, Rawalpindi") is the main central business district of [Rawalpindi](/wiki/Rawalpindi "Rawalpindi").

## Peru

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Peru")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bf/Sanisidroskyscrapers.jpg/250px-Sanisidroskyscrapers.jpg)](/wiki/File:Sanisidroskyscrapers.jpg)

Central business district of [Lima](/wiki/Lima "Lima"), Peru

In [Peru](/wiki/Peru "Peru"), the central business district is [San Isidro](/wiki/San_Isidro,_Peru "San Isidro, Peru"), in [Lima](/wiki/Lima "Lima"), which hosts the majority of Peru's financial industry headquarters.[[12]](#cite_note-12) Although still a largely residential district, the commercial and business activity located in or in the vicinity of the area.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14) It has[*[when?](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Dates_and_numbers#Chronological_items "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers")*] a permanent population of around 63,000 inhabitants[[15]](#cite_note-15) and, during weekday business hours, a floating population that exceeds 700,000 daily commuters from other districts in [Lima](/wiki/Lima "Lima"), the national capital.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

[San Isidro](/wiki/San_Isidro,_Peru "San Isidro, Peru") is served by three stations of [El Metropolitano](/wiki/El_Metropolitano "El Metropolitano"), Lima's [bus rapid transit](/wiki/Bus_rapid_transit "Bus rapid transit") system: [Estación Javier Prado](/wiki/Javier_Prado_(Metropolitano) "Javier Prado (Metropolitano)"), [Estación Canaval y Moreyra](/wiki/Canaval_y_Moreyra_(Metropolitano) "Canaval y Moreyra (Metropolitano)") (with over 16,000 daily passengers)[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18) and [Estación Aramburú](/wiki/Arambur%C3%BA_(Metropolitano) "Aramburú (Metropolitano)").

In the early 21st century, the southeastern district of [Surco](/wiki/Surco "Surco")[[19]](#cite_note-19) experienced a significant increase in upscale corporate developments in the area comprised by avenues Manuel Holguín, El Derby, El Polo, and La Encalada due to lower restrictions to grant [construction permit](/wiki/Construction_permit "Construction permit") and proximity to residential middle and upper class districts and is set to become, after traditional [San Isidro](/wiki/San_Isidro,_Peru "San Isidro, Peru") and [Miraflores](/wiki/Miraflores_District,_Lima "Miraflores District, Lima"), the new [financial center](/wiki/Financial_center "Financial center") of [Lima](/wiki/Lima "Lima").

The Philippines has three major central business districts, which are all located in Metro Manila. Bonifacio Global City is the newest and one of the largest central business districts in the Philippines. The Bases Conversion and Development Authority (BCDA), Ayala Land, Inc. and Evergreen Holdings, Inc. controls Fort Bonifacio Development Corporation, which oversees the master planning of Bonifacio Global City. Ortigas Center, with an area of more than 100 hectares (250 acres), is the Philippines' second most important business district after the Makati CBD and is home to Asian Development Bank. Meanwhile, the Makati Central Business District, also known as the Makati CBD, is the leading financial and the largest central business district in the Philippines.

The largest central business district in [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia") is the [Moscow International Business Center](/wiki/Moscow_International_Business_Center "Moscow International Business Center") (MIBC) in [Moscow](/wiki/Moscow "Moscow"), a commercial development located just east of the [Third Ring Road](/wiki/Third_Ring_Road_(Moscow) "Third Ring Road (Moscow)") at the western edge of the [Presnensky District](/wiki/Presnensky_District "Presnensky District") in the [Central Administrative Okrug](/wiki/Central_Administrative_Okrug "Central Administrative Okrug") of the city. As of 2021, it is still under development. The construction of the MIBC takes place on the Presnenskaya Embankment of the [Moskva River](/wiki/Moskva_(river) "Moskva (river)"), approximately 4 kilometers (2.5 mi) west of [Red Square](/wiki/Red_Square "Red Square"), overlooked by the Third Ring Road. The project occupies an area of 60 hectares.

The second-largest central business district is [Yekaterinburg-City](/wiki/Yekaterinburg-City "Yekaterinburg-City") in [Yekaterinburg](/wiki/Yekaterinburg "Yekaterinburg"), the [fourth-largest city](/wiki/List_of_cities_and_towns_in_Russia_by_population "List of cities and towns in Russia by population") in the country. It is located on the [Iset River](/wiki/Iset_(river) "Iset (river)"), and is under development as of 2021. The area occupies five hectares.

## Singapore

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Singapore")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/82/1_Singapore_city_skyline_2010_day_panorama.jpg/250px-1_Singapore_city_skyline_2010_day_panorama.jpg)](/wiki/File:1_Singapore_city_skyline_2010_day_panorama.jpg)

The Singapore River Planning Area seen from the eastern bank of the [Singapore River](/wiki/Singapore_River "Singapore River") with the central business district located on its western bank

In [Singapore](/wiki/Singapore "Singapore"), the CBD is in the [Downtown Core](/wiki/Downtown_Core "Downtown Core"), one of the constituent [planning areas](/wiki/Planning_Areas_of_Singapore "Planning Areas of Singapore") of the [Central Area](/wiki/Central_Area,_Singapore "Central Area, Singapore"), the country's [city center](/wiki/City_center "City center"). Its densest point is centered around [Raffles Place](/wiki/Raffles_Place "Raffles Place"), where most of Singapore's skyscrapers are located. The CBD sometimes also describes Central Area as a whole.

Singapore's CBD is the historic heart of the city-state, which makes it a mix of a tourist attraction and a business center. The CBD includes several multinational corporate headquarters, [Singapore Management University](/wiki/Singapore_Management_University "Singapore Management University"), and historical buildings and museums.

As of 2016, the [Singaporean government](/wiki/Government_of_Singapore "Government of Singapore") intends to redevelop [Jurong East](/wiki/Jurong_East "Jurong East") into a second CBD in Singapore.[[20]](#cite_note-20) The area has also been earmarked as the site of the rail terminus for the [Kuala Lumpur–Singapore High Speed Rail](/wiki/Kuala_Lumpur%E2%80%93Singapore_High_Speed_Rail "Kuala Lumpur–Singapore High Speed Rail").

## South Africa

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: South Africa")]

[South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa")'s largest cities - [Cape Town](/wiki/Cape_Town "Cape Town"), [Durban](/wiki/Durban "Durban"), [Johannesburg](/wiki/Johannesburg "Johannesburg"), [Pretoria](/wiki/Pretoria "Pretoria"), and [Port Elizabeth](/wiki/Port_Elizabeth "Port Elizabeth") - all have CBDs. The country's CBDs are home to the majority of the corporate headquarters of South Africa's largest companies as well as foreign institutions. Its CBDs are also home to major [convention centers](/wiki/Convention_center "Convention center"), [tourist attractions](/wiki/Tourism_in_South_Africa "Tourism in South Africa"), and many of the country's tallest buildings.

[Cape Town CBD](/wiki/Cape_Town_CBD "Cape Town CBD") has one of South Africa's most iconic skylines. The CBD sits in the [City Bowl](/wiki/City_Bowl "City Bowl") region, below [Table Mountain](/wiki/Table_Mountain "Table Mountain"), and next to the [V&A Waterfront](/wiki/V%26A_Waterfront "V&A Waterfront"), and the [Port of Cape Town](/wiki/Port_of_Cape_Town "Port of Cape Town").[[21]](#cite_note-21)

## South Korea

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: South Korea")]

Further information: [Downtown Seoul](/wiki/Downtown_Seoul "Downtown Seoul"), [Gangnam](/wiki/Gangnam "Gangnam"), and [Yeouido](/wiki/Yeouido "Yeouido")

In [South Korea](/wiki/South_Korea "South Korea"), the national capital of [Seoul](/wiki/Seoul "Seoul") includes three central business districts. The [Downtown Seoul](/wiki/Downtown_Seoul "Downtown Seoul"), a historic area inside the [Fortress Wall of Seoul](/wiki/Fortress_Wall_of_Seoul "Fortress Wall of Seoul") in the [Jongno](/wiki/Jongno_District "Jongno District") and [Jung](/wiki/Jung_District,_Seoul "Jung District, Seoul") districts has historically been the city's political, social and cultural heart, and still houses many corporate headquarters and political institutions in South Korea. It is also the most popular area for tourists and the city's main commercial district. While the district has a high density of high rise buildings, the tallest building, the SK Building, only reaches a height of 160m due to historic preservation regulations.[[22]](#cite_note-22): 134–135

The second main district, located in the southeast part of Seoul, is [Gangnam District](/wiki/Gangnam_District "Gangnam District"), an area that was developed in the 1980s and is now among the city's most affluent neighborhoods. The Gangnam District has concentration mainly in economic power of Seoul, as many of prominent company HQs are located on [Teheran Avenue](/wiki/Teheran-ro "Teheran-ro"). Gangnam Avenue and Yeondong Avenue, which includes the [COEX](/wiki/COEX "COEX") mall, are major thoroughfares in Seoul. The tallest buildings in Gangnam include [Tower Palace One](/wiki/Tower_Palace_One "Tower Palace One"), a residential complex, [Trade Tower](/wiki/Trade_Tower "Trade Tower"), Gangnam Finance Center, and Parnas Tower.

Seoul's third business district is [Yeouido](/wiki/Yeouido "Yeouido"), an island located in central Seoul in the [Yeongdeungpo District](/wiki/Yeongdeungpo_District "Yeongdeungpo District"). Yeouido was developed in the 1970s; since the 1990s, however, it has become Seoul's financial center. Smaller in geographic size that the other two business districts, Yeouido has some of Seoul's tallest skyscrapers, including [Parc1 Tower](/wiki/Parc1_Tower "Parc1 Tower"), [International Finance Center Seoul](/wiki/International_Finance_Center_Seoul "International Finance Center Seoul"), and the [63 Building](/wiki/63_Building "63 Building"). Yeouido is where the [National Assembly of South Korea](/wiki/National_Assembly_of_South_Korea "National Assembly of South Korea") and many media companies and political institutions are based.

Smaller but notable central business districts in South Korea include the [Jamsil](/wiki/Jamsil-dong "Jamsil-dong") area, which is the location of the 555-metre tall [Lotte World Tower](/wiki/Lotte_World_Tower "Lotte World Tower"), [Guro](/wiki/Guro-dong "Guro-dong") Digital Complex, [Gasan](/wiki/Gasan-dong "Gasan-dong") Digital Complex, the [Magok](/w/index.php?title=Magok&action=edit&redlink=1 "Magok (page does not exist)") Business District, the [Sangam](/wiki/Sangam-dong "Sangam-dong") Digital Media City, the [Munjeong](/wiki/Munjeong-dong "Munjeong-dong") Administrative Town, and Mapo Avenue. The area near [Yongsan Station](/wiki/Yongsan_Station "Yongsan Station"), including the proposed [Yongsan International Business District](/wiki/Yongsan_International_Business_District "Yongsan International Business District"), is considered an emerging major commercial district that will exist in synergy with nearby [Yeouido](/wiki/Yeouido "Yeouido").

In [Busan](/wiki/Busan "Busan"), South Korea's second-largest city, the central business district is along the Jungang Avenue, starting around the commercial area of [Seomyeon Station](/wiki/Seomyeon_Station "Seomyeon Station") and then proceeding south to the [Busan International Finance Center](/wiki/Busan_International_Finance_Center "Busan International Finance Center") in Munhyeon-dong and the area near [Busan Station](/wiki/Busan_Station "Busan Station") and Jungang-dong, where several corporate offices are located. A second major business district in Busan is Centum City, in the [Haeundae District](/wiki/Haeundae_District "Haeundae District").

## Spain

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Spain")]

In [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain"), the largest central business districts are in [Madrid](/wiki/Madrid "Madrid"), the national capital. [Paseo de la Castellana](/wiki/Paseo_de_la_Castellana "Paseo de la Castellana") includes the city's main business districts: the [Gate of Europe](/wiki/Gate_of_Europe "Gate of Europe"), [AZCA](/wiki/AZCA "AZCA"), and [CTBA](/wiki/Cuatro_Torres_Business_Area "Cuatro Torres Business Area"). AZCA is 19-hectare (47-acre) super block near [Real Madrid's](/wiki/Real_Madrid_C.F. "Real Madrid C.F.") stadium [Santiago Bernabéu](/wiki/Santiago_Bernab%C3%A9u_Stadium "Santiago Bernabéu Stadium"). It was the country's main business area during the late 20th century when most of the region's skyscrapers were developed. The tallest building in AZCA is the [Torre Picasso](/wiki/Torre_Picasso "Torre Picasso"), a 158-storey tower[*[clarification needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*] that was designed by [Minoru Yamasaki](/wiki/Minoru_Yamasaki "Minoru Yamasaki") architect, and includes the national headquarters for several multinational corporations, including [Google](/wiki/Google "Google") and [Deloitte](/wiki/Deloitte "Deloitte").

There is also [Madrid Nuevo Norte](/wiki/Madrid_Nuevo_Norte "Madrid Nuevo Norte"), starting construction in 2025. Making it one of the largest urban rehabilitation projects in Europe. It will include a new Central Business District, connecting with the already constructed [Cuatro Torres Business Area](/wiki/Cuatro_Torres_Business_Area "Cuatro Torres Business Area").[[23]](#cite_note-23)

The Gate of Europe has twin towers, which hold Spanish bank [Bankia](/wiki/Bankia "Bankia") and Realia, a real estate company. A few blocks north of the Gate of Europe is CTBA, a complex of four skyscrapers that are the tallest in Spain. Notable architects, including [Norman Foster](/wiki/Norman_Foster,_Baron_Foster_of_Thames_Bank "Norman Foster, Baron Foster of Thames Bank"), [Ieoh Ming Pei](/wiki/Ieoh_Ming_Pei "Ieoh Ming Pei"), and [Cesar Pelli](/wiki/Cesar_Pelli "Cesar Pelli"), have designed its towers, which were completed in 2008. The tallest building, [Torre de Cristal](/wiki/Torre_de_Cristal "Torre de Cristal"), is the fourth-tallest building is [Western Europe](/wiki/Western_Europe "Western Europe") with a height of 250 meters (820 ft). The complex houses the Spain-based headquarters for several multinational corporations, including [KPMG](/wiki/KPMG "KPMG"), [Coca-Cola](/wiki/Coca-Cola "Coca-Cola"), [Volkswagen](/wiki/Volkswagen "Volkswagen"), [Bankia](/wiki/Bankia "Bankia"), [Cepsa](/wiki/Cepsa "Cepsa"), [PwC](/wiki/PwC "PwC"), [OHL](/wiki/Obrasc%C3%B3n_Huarte_Lain "Obrascón Huarte Lain"), and the embassies of the [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands"), [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"), [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada") and [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"), and a five-star hotel owned by [Eurostars](/wiki/Eurostars_Hotels "Eurostars Hotels"). A fifth tower is being for [Instituto de Empresa](/wiki/Instituto_de_Empresa "Instituto de Empresa") university.

In [Barcelona](/wiki/Barcelona "Barcelona"), the [22@](/wiki/22@ "22@"), [Gran Via](/wiki/Gran_Via_(Barcelona) "Gran Via (Barcelona)") and [Granvia l'Hospitalet](/wiki/Granvia_l%27Hospitalet "Granvia l'Hospitalet") are the city's two main business districts. The Catalan capital does not have a reputation for skyscrapers and financial hubs, but has attracted several media and technology companies, including [Microsoft](/wiki/Microsoft "Microsoft") and [Yahoo!](/wiki/Yahoo! "Yahoo!"). In 2005, the [Torre Agbar](/wiki/Torre_Agbar "Torre Agbar"), designed by French architect [Jean Nouvel](/wiki/Jean_Nouvel "Jean Nouvel"), is the third-tallest building in Barcelona with a height of 145 meters (476 ft) and was intended to become a [Hyatt](/wiki/Hyatt "Hyatt") hotel once administrative issues are resolved.[[24]](#cite_note-lavanguardia-24)

Moreover, [Barcelona](/wiki/Barcelona "Barcelona") is building the new [La Sagrera](/wiki/La_Sagrera "La Sagrera") station which will include a new office buildings, parks and culture centers. Construction has already started by moving the railway tracks in the area underground.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

## Taiwan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Taiwan")]

In [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan"), the term "city centre" ([Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): 市中心) is often used instead of central business district, but a different commercial district outside of the historic core is typically labelled a CBD ([Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): 中心商業區), "financial district" ([Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): 金融貿易區), or "Yolk area" ([Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): 蛋黃區). Taiwan also has many traditional central business districts.

In [Taipei](/wiki/Taipei "Taipei"), the capital city of [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan"), the area around its [main railway station](/wiki/Taipei_Station "Taipei Station"), including the [Taipei West District Gateway Project](/wiki/Taipei_West_District_Gateway_Project "Taipei West District Gateway Project"), is regarded as Taipei's historic centre; it is home to many of Taiwan's national government buildings, including the [Presidential Office](/wiki/Presidential_Office_Building,_Taipei "Presidential Office Building, Taipei"), the [Executive Yuan](/wiki/Executive_Yuan "Executive Yuan"), the [Control Yuan](/wiki/Control_Yuan "Control Yuan"), the [Legislative Yuan](/wiki/Legislative_Yuan "Legislative Yuan"), the [Judicial Yuan](/wiki/Judicial_Yuan "Judicial Yuan"), and various government ministries.

The second main district, in the central part Taipei, is the [Xinyi Special District](/wiki/Xinyi_Special_District "Xinyi Special District"), which was developed mostly in the 1990s, and has emerged as Taipei's financial, political, and commercial center. The district includes the greatest concentration of corporate headquarters and offices in Taipei and is amongst the nation's largest shopping districts. It also includes many of Taipei's tallest skyscrapers, including the 509.2-meter (1,671 ft)-tall [Taipei 101](/wiki/Taipei_101 "Taipei 101"), [Taipei Nan Shan Plaza](/wiki/Taipei_Nan_Shan_Plaza "Taipei Nan Shan Plaza"), and [Fubon Xinyi A25](/wiki/Fubon_Xinyi_A25 "Fubon Xinyi A25").[[26]](#cite_note-Xinyi_Commercial_District-26)

Taipei's third business district, [Nangang District](/wiki/Nangang_District,_Taipei "Nangang District, Taipei"), is situated in the very east of the city. Nangang was developed in the early 21st century and is the seat of the [Academia Sinica](/wiki/Academia_Sinica "Academia Sinica"), [Taipei World Trade Center Nangang Exhibition Hall](/wiki/Taipei_World_Trade_Center_Nangang_Exhibition_Hall "Taipei World Trade Center Nangang Exhibition Hall"), and [Nankang Software Park](/wiki/Nankang_Software_Park "Nankang Software Park") (NKSP).

[Greater Taipei](/wiki/Greater_Taipei "Greater Taipei") serves as a multi-central [metropolitan area](/wiki/Metropolitan_area "Metropolitan area"), and a few other areas have become important business districts, including [Daan District](/wiki/Daan_District,_Taipei "Daan District, Taipei"), [Xinban Special District](/wiki/Xinban_Special_District "Xinban Special District"), [Neihu District](/wiki/Neihu_District "Neihu District") and [Xinzhuang Sub-city Center](/wiki/Xinzhuang_Sub-city_Center "Xinzhuang Sub-city Center"), which is considered to have the potential to emerge as a major commercial district in Taiwan, functioning in synergy with nearby [Sanchong District](/wiki/Sanchong_District "Sanchong District") and [Wugu District](/wiki/Wugu_District "Wugu District").

In [Taichung](/wiki/Taichung "Taichung"), Taiwan's second-largest city after Taipei, the central business district is [Taichung's 7th Redevelopment Zone](/wiki/Taichung%27s_7th_Redevelopment_Zone "Taichung's 7th Redevelopment Zone"). It features broad and widely spaced boulevards, attractive apartments, department stores, and office towers, which are brightly lit at night.

In [Kaohsiung](/wiki/Kaohsiung "Kaohsiung"), Taiwan's harbour city, the central business district is [Asia New Bay Area](/wiki/Asia_New_Bay_Area "Asia New Bay Area"), which is home to the city's tallest skyscrapers, including [85 Sky Tower](/wiki/85_Sky_Tower "85 Sky Tower") and [Farglory THE ONE](/wiki/Farglory_THE_ONE "Farglory THE ONE") and [Port of Kaohsiung](/wiki/Port_of_Kaohsiung "Port of Kaohsiung"), the nation's largest port.

## United Kingdom

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_business_district&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: United Kingdom")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/86/City_of_London%2C_seen_from_Tower_Bridge.jpg/250px-City_of_London%2C_seen_from_Tower_Bridge.jpg)](/wiki/File:City_of_London,_seen_from_Tower_Bridge.jpg)

The [City of London](/wiki/City_of_London "City of London"), as seen from [Tower Bridge](/wiki/Tower_Bridge "Tower Bridge").

The [City of London](/wiki/City_of_London "City of London") and [Canary Wharf](/wiki/Canary_Wharf "Canary Wharf") serve as not only England's, but the entire United Kingdom's largest (via economic output) CBDs. The City holds the crown of the vastest concentration of economic output in the world.[[28]](#cite_note-28)

London and Canary Wharf house a significant number of the world's largest corporations. In the 2023 [Fortune 500 Europe list](/wiki/Fortune_500 "Fortune 500"), London had 44 companies, making up about 9% of the list's revenue. Specifically, Shell and BP are headquartered in London, with other notable companies like Unilever and GSK also being based there. Canary Wharf, a major financial district in London, is also home to many global financial institutions, including [HSBC](/wiki/HSBC "HSBC"), [Citigroup](/wiki/Citigroup "Citigroup"), [Wells Fargo](/wiki/Wells_Fargo "Wells Fargo"), [Barclays](/wiki/Barclays "Barclays"), [JPMorgan Chase](/wiki/JPMorgan_Chase "JPMorgan Chase"), [MetLife](/wiki/MetLife "MetLife"), [Clifford Chance](/wiki/Clifford_Chance "Clifford Chance"), and [Morgan Stanley](/wiki/Morgan_Stanley "Morgan Stanley").[[29]](#cite_note-29)
